pages.raw_text:
Grade separation along the right
of way of the Northern Pacific in
this city is not dead issue by
any means. President Howard FL
lott Is in town, and in his own
words is willing to ''try to be fair;
the Northern Pacific is trying to
do its part"


President Elliott was closeted
this morning with several leading
business men. but the utmost Sc
crecy has been exercised in cover
ing up the details of the session.
One rumor has been started to the
effect that the company intended
to abandon its tracks and enter the
city over the Spokane, Portland &
Seattle right of way, but this is
given little credence.


That grade separation is an OBJ
ject of President Elliott's visit to
Spokane Is now conceded to this
extent:


The former opposition has dwin-
died down largely to a matter of
Cowles Discrimination enlarged by
unlimited publicity facilities.


A number of big men in the
chamber of commerce are not rab-
idly opposed to couple of millions
worth of work being done in Spo-
kane this year Mr.. Elliott is will
ing to hear what they have to say.
He leaves tonight


President Elliott was the guest
of the chamber of commerce at a
banquet last night, and while he
sat at the board he was the target
for enough hints as to lower rates
for Spokane to last him several
months. The majority of the speak
ers mentioned the aims of Spokane
and how these could be attained in
measure by lower freight rates.


When President Elliott respond
ed he made no direct reference to
the appeal, but he did elaborate
to some extent on the public agita-
ton against the railroads in gen.
eral.
